seoTitle: How to Set Up MLA Format in Google Docs (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Set up an MLA-format paper in Google Docs with 1-inch margins, Times New Roman 12pt, double spacing, a running header, and a proper Works Cited page.
intro: MLA formatting has strict, specific rules, and getting them right in Google Docs takes more than eyeballing it. This guide covers configuring margins, font, and spacing to MLA standards, building your identification block and running header, and setting up a Works Cited page with proper hanging indents, so your paper meets academic requirements from the first page.

steps.heading: Set 1-inch margins
steps.body: Open Page Setup and lock all four document margins to exactly 1.0 inch around your text canvas.

steps.heading: Set font and size
steps.body: Switch your font family to Times New Roman at 12pt, the standard accepted size for MLA papers.

steps.heading: Apply double spacing
steps.body: Use the line spacing dashboard to set the entire document to double spacing, and set paragraph before/after spacing to zero.

steps.heading: Build your identification block
steps.body: Type your Student Name, Professor's Name, Course Title, and Date in the top-left corner, then center your essay title below it.

steps.heading: Add the running header
steps.body: Double-click the top margin to insert your last name alongside an automated page number in the top-right corner.

steps.heading: Set paragraph indents
steps.body: Use the horizontal ruler to configure a half-inch first-line indent for each body paragraph.

steps.heading: Format your Works Cited page
steps.body: Start a new page for sources and apply a 0.5-inch hanging indent to each citation entry.

faq.question: What margin size does MLA format require?
faq.answer: MLA format requires exactly 1.0 inch margins on all four sides of the page.

faq.question: Does MLA require a title page?
faq.answer: No, MLA format typically uses a top-left identification block on the first page instead of a separate title page.

faq.question: How do I add my last name and page number in the header?
faq.answer: Double-click the top margin to open the header area, then type your last name followed by an automated page number token.

faq.question: What is a hanging indent and why does my Works Cited page need one?
faq.answer: A hanging indent keeps the first line of each citation flush left while wrapping lines are indented, which is the required format for MLA source listings.
